title: sorbus suecica
intro: Sorbus suecica, also known as the Swedish whitebeam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to northern Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's berries have been used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and respiratory problems, due to their high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. Herbal preparations of Sorbus suecica, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation in traditional folk medicine.
